title: Yens Pedersen

infobox.value: is a Canadian politician and lawyer, who served in the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") from 2018 to 2020 representing the constituency of [Regina Northeast](/wiki/Regina_Northeast "Regina Northeast"). He is a former candidate in the [Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party") leadership race. Before entering the leadership race, he served as the president of the Saskatchewan New Democratic Party.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian politician and lawyer

| Yens Pedersen | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Member of the [Saskatchewan Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Legislative_Assembly "Saskatchewan Legislative Assembly") for [Regina Northeast](/wiki/Regina_Northeast "Regina Northeast") | |
| **In office** September 12, 2018 – September 29, 2020 | |
| Preceded by | [Kevin Doherty](/wiki/Kevin_Doherty_(politician) "Kevin Doherty (politician)") |
| Succeeded by | [Gary Grewal](/wiki/Gary_Grewal "Gary Grewal") |
|  | |
| Personal details | |
| [Party](/wiki/Political_party "Political party") | [New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party") |
| Occupation | Lawyer |
|  | |

**Yens Pedersen** is a Canadian politician and lawyer, who served in the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan") from 2018 to 2020 representing the constituency of [Regina Northeast](/wiki/Regina_Northeast "Regina Northeast"). He is a former candidate in the [Saskatchewan New Democratic Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party") leadership race. Before entering the leadership race, he served as the president of the Saskatchewan New Democratic Party.

## Early life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yens_Pedersen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and career")]

Pedersen was raised on a family farm near [Cut Knife, Saskatchewan](/wiki/Cut_Knife,_Saskatchewan "Cut Knife, Saskatchewan"). He attended and earned his law degree from [Osgoode Hall Law School](/wiki/Osgoode_Hall_Law_School "Osgoode Hall Law School") and has been a Regina lawyer for over 20 years, specializing in work with individual families and small to mid-size businesses. He has been the chair of the Taxation section of the Canadian Bar Association in Regina and also the president of the Regina Business Association. He also served on the board of directors for Saskatchewan Express (musical theatre group) and for The Royal Lifesaving Society (Saskatchewan Branch).[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Political career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yens_Pedersen&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Political career")]

Pedersen was the NDP candidate in the constituency of [Regina South](/wiki/Regina_South "Regina South") in the [2007](/wiki/2007_Saskatchewan_general_election "2007 Saskatchewan general election") and [2011](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election") provincial elections.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In both elections, he placed second to the [Bill Hutchinson](/wiki/Bill_Hutchinson_(politician) "Bill Hutchinson (politician)") of the [Saskatchewan Party](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Party "Saskatchewan Party").

On January 22, 2009, Pedersen announced his bid to succeed [Lorne Calvert](/wiki/Lorne_Calvert "Lorne Calvert") as the leader of the Saskatchewan NDP at the party's [June 2009 leadership convention](/wiki/Saskatchewan_New_Democratic_Party/Co-operative_Commonwealth_Federation_leadership_conventions#2009_New_Democratic_Party_leadership_convention "Saskatchewan New Democratic Party/Co-operative Commonwealth Federation leadership conventions").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He finished third at the convention. After [Deb Higgins](/wiki/Deb_Higgins "Deb Higgins") was eliminated on the first ballot, Pedersen threw his support behind [Ryan Meili](/wiki/Ryan_Meili "Ryan Meili"), the second place finisher on the first ballot.

On May 2, 2018, Pedersen won the NDP nomination for an upcoming provincial by-election in [Regina Northeast](/wiki/Regina_Northeast "Regina Northeast").[[4]](#cite_note-4) He won the by-election with 54% of the vote on September 12, 2018.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
